mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equities, compressing valuations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration, reducing corporate earnings expectations and triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or signal accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that immediately pressures equity valuations through earnings downgrade expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of contraction periods during tightening cycles, invalidating the short-term signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a lagging federal funds rate creates a negative real rate environment that forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods yield positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumption capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive forward equity returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth vs 60-day returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or signal flips negative.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth signals economic contraction that immediately reduces equity risk premiums and triggers negative forward returns as investors price in recessionary earnings.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of historical instances, breaking the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad-based economic slowdown, accelerating capital flight from equities and generating negative short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Concurrent supply contraction and labor weakening fails to predict negative 5-day returns with >55% accuracy in walk-forward tests.
